serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of clams porridge weighs approximately 400 g, representing a typical single-serve portion as served in Singaporean Vietnamese restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Congee with seafood (rice porridge with seafood) — adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central clam and rice porridge entries
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry for clam porridge (Chao Ngheu) was found. Values are adapted from HPB Singapore seafood congee / rice porridge entries combined with USDA FoodData Central data for cooked clams (NDB 15157) and plain rice porridge. The dish is approximately 85% rice porridge base and 15% clam meat by weight. Weighted composite used: rice porridge per 100g (~55 kcal, 1.2g protein, 0.2g fat, 12g carb) blended with cooked clam contribution (~148 kcal, 25.6g protein, 2.0g fat, 5.1g carb per 100g clam meat). Final per-100g values reflect the mixed dish at typical restaurant dilution.

gi_source.title: Congee / rice porridge GI — analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for clam porridge (Chao Ngheu). GI of 68 is an analogous estimate based on rice congee / plain rice porridge entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables, which report GI values for rice porridge/congee in the range of 57–78 (mean ~68). The addition of clam protein and fat slightly lowers the effective GI; a conservative mid-range value of 68 is used. GL per serving calculated as (68 × 46g available carbohydrate per 400g serving) / 100 = 31.3, rounded to 31.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from fish sauce and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: A 400 g serving provides approximately 1280 mg sodium (53% of the 2400 mg daily limit), so individuals with hypertension should request reduced fish sauce and seasoning and limit portion size to 200–250 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index rice porridge base with moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 68 and a GL of ~31 per standard bowl, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a bowl (200 g), pair with additional protein or non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response, and monitor blood glucose after consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat with beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from clams
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Clams are a low-saturated-fat shellfish providing omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin B12, which are cardioprotective; however, the sodium content from fish sauce warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease, and portion control is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Clams Porridge (Chao Ngheu) is a Vietnamese-style rice congee dish popular in Singapore, particularly at Vietnamese restaurants such as Thien Long. The dish is prepared by slow-simmering white rice in water or light stock until it breaks down into a smooth, thick porridge, then adding fresh short-neck clams (ngheu) that are briefly cooked until just opened. The porridge is seasoned with fish sauce, salt, white pepper, and garnished with sliced ginger, spring onion, and sometimes coriander. The texture is silky and comforting, with a mild briny sweetness from the clams. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in calories (~72 kcal per 100g), relatively high in protein from the clams (~4.8g per 100g), low in fat (0.7g per 100g), and provides a meaningful source of iron (5.2 mg per 100g), vitamin B12 (11 mcg per 100g), and selenium. The sodium content is elevated due to fish sauce seasoning (~320 mg per 100g), making it a moderate concern for hypertension. The glycemic index is approximately 68 (medium), driven by the white rice base. Clinically, it is suitable for most diets in moderate portions, with particular attention needed for sodium intake in hypertensive individuals and glycemic load management in those with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish reflects Vietnamese culinary tradition adapted to the Singaporean hawker and restaurant context.
common_user_queries: How many calories in clams porridge?
Is clam congee good for diabetics?
What is the sodium content of clam porridge?
What are the ingredients in Chao Ngheu?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry for clam porridge (Chao Ngheu) was found. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore seafood congee entries and USDA FoodData Central cooked clam data using a weighted composite approach. GI is an analogous estimate from rice porridge/congee entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the dish composition is well-understood.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on HPB Singapore seafood congee and USDA FoodData Central cooked clam (NDB 15157) data; no exact entry for Chao Ngheu exists in any approved primary source. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB or USDA source entries used for this adaptation. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the source entries used for this adaptation. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as clams and plain rice porridge contain no industrially produced trans fats; naturally zero for this dish. 5. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate from rice porridge/congee entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008); no direct GI measurement for clam porridge exists. 6. Halal status is marked false as clams are considered non-halal in the Shafi'i school of Islamic jurisprudence followed in Singapore; consumers should verify with the serving restaurant. 7. The dish is served at Thien Long Vietnam Restaurant, Circular Road, Singapore, as identified in the GrabFood source record.
